```json
{
  "domain_analysis": {
    "business_context": "A global logistics platform adding a driver-facing earnings transparency feature across three jurisdictions with fundamentally different labor classifications, legal requirements, and governance structures. This is not a single feature — it is three jurisdiction-specific implementations unified by a shared UI concept. The domain complexity is primarily legal and regulatory, not technical.",
    "bounded_context": "Driver Earnings / Compensation Transparency",
    "ubiquitous_language": [
      { "term": "Base Pay", "definition": "The guaranteed compensation a driver receives for accepting and completing a delivery, excluding bonuses, tips, and incentives. In employee jurisdictions (CA), this must meet or exceed minimum wage independently of tips." },
      { "term": "Per-Delivery Bonus", "definition": "Variable compensation tied to completing individual deliveries, which may include surge pricing, distance premiums, or priority order incentives." },
      { "term": "Tip", "definition": "A voluntary gratuity from the customer to the driver. Legally distinct from wages in employee jurisdictions — must never subsidize or offset base pay." },
      { "term": "Deduction", "definition": "Any amount subtracted from gross earnings. Meaning is jurisdiction-dependent: tax withholding and benefits for employees (CA); potentially social contributions in Germany; platform commission/service fees for independent contractors (Indonesia)." },
      { "term": "Earnings Breakdown", "definition": "An itemized view of all compensation components and deductions for a given period, summing to a net earnings figure." },
      { "term": "Works Council (Betriebsrat)", "definition": "A legally mandated employee representation body in Germany with co-determination rights over changes to compensation display and monitoring systems. Their approval is a legal prerequisite, not a courtesy." },
      { "term": "AB5 Classification", "definition": "California Assembly Bill 5, which classifies gig workers as employees unless the hiring entity passes the ABC test. Triggers full wage-and-hour protections including itemized wage statement requirements." },
      { "term": "Itemized Wage Statement", "definition": "Under California Labor Code §226, employers must provide detailed pay stubs showing gross wages, total hours, deductions, net wages, pay period, and employer identity. The earnings breakdown must satisfy this legal instrument." },
      { "term": "Net Earnings", "definition": "The final amount a driver receives after all deductions are applied to gross earnings. Must always equal the sum of all positive line items minus all deductions." },
      { "term": "Driver Classification", "definition": "The legal labor status of a driver — employee or independent contractor — which determines which wage laws, tax obligations, and transparency requirements apply." }
    ]
  },

  "business_rules": [
    {
      "id": "BR-1",
      "rule": "The net earnings figure displayed must always equal the arithmetic sum of all visible line items (base pay + bonuses + tips - deductions). No hidden components are permitted.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "If the displayed breakdown does not sum to the displayed total, the system is presenting corrupt or misleading financial data. This is a data integrity constraint — a driver seeing numbers that don't add up destroys trust and may constitute fraud.",
      "testable_assertion": "For any earnings breakdown displayed, SUM(positive_components) - SUM(deductions) == displayed_net_earnings, with zero tolerance for rounding beyond the smallest currency unit."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-2",
      "rule": "In California, tips must be displayed as a separate line item and must never be counted toward base pay or used to meet minimum wage obligations.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "California Labor Code §351 prohibits employers from crediting tips against minimum wage. Commingling tips with base pay in the display — or in the underlying calculation — violates state labor law and exposes the platform to class-action liability.",
      "testable_assertion": "In the California earnings breakdown, a 'Tips' line item exists independently, and the 'Base Pay' value is >= (hours_worked * applicable_minimum_wage) without including any tip amount."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-3",
      "rule": "In California, the earnings breakdown must satisfy all requirements of an itemized wage statement under Labor Code §226: gross wages earned, total hours worked, all deductions with descriptions, net wages, pay period dates, and employer legal name and address.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Failure to provide compliant itemized wage statements in California carries statutory penalties of $50-$100 per violation per employee, plus actual damages. This is a legal compliance constraint that cannot be relaxed by business policy.",
      "testable_assertion": "Every California earnings breakdown includes: gross wages, total hours, itemized deductions with labels, net wages, inclusive dates of the pay period, and employer legal entity name and address."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-4",
      "rule": "The earnings transparency feature must not be deployed to drivers in Germany until explicit written approval from the applicable Works Council (Betriebsrat) has been obtained and recorded.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Under the German Works Constitution Act (BetrVG §87), the works council has co-determination rights over the introduction of technical systems that monitor employee performance or compensation. Deploying without approval is a legal violation that can result in injunctions and the feature being forcibly disabled.",
      "testable_assertion": "The feature flag for Germany earnings transparency cannot be enabled unless a works_council_approval record exists with status 'approved' and a valid approval date."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-5",
      "rule": "In Indonesia, platform commission and service fees must be displayed as deductions from the driver's gross earnings, clearly labeled as platform fees — not as wage deductions.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "This is a business transparency policy for independent contractors. Indonesian law does not currently mandate this specific display format, but the business has chosen to show it. The labeling convention could be changed by product leadership.",
      "testable_assertion": "Indonesian driver earnings breakdowns label platform-retained amounts as 'Platform Fee' or 'Service Fee', not as 'Deduction' or 'Withholding'."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-6",
      "rule": "Deduction categories must be jurisdiction-specific. Employee jurisdictions show tax withholding, social contributions, and benefits. Contractor jurisdictions show platform fees and commissions. These categories must never be mixed.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Showing tax withholding deductions to an independent contractor (or platform fees to an employee) implies an incorrect legal relationship. This misclassification in the UI could be used as evidence in labor classification disputes.",
      "testable_assertion": "A California driver never sees a 'Platform Fee' deduction. An Indonesian driver never sees a 'Tax Withholding' or 'Benefits' deduction. Each jurisdiction renders only its legally appropriate deduction categories."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-7",
      "rule": "All monetary values in the earnings breakdown must be displayed in the local currency of the driver's operating jurisdiction with locale-appropriate formatting.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Displaying earnings in a foreign currency or with incorrect decimal/thousands separators constitutes a misrepresentation of compensation. Drivers make financial decisions based on these figures.",

    },

      "rule": "Earnings data must reflect confirmed, finalized transactions only. Pending or estimated amounts must be visually distinguished and labeled as such.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Displaying unconfirmed earnings as finalized creates a false compensation record. If a tip is later adjusted or a delivery disputed, the driver has relied on inaccurate data. This is a data integrity and trust constraint.",
      "testable_assertion": "Any earnings component not yet finalized (e.g., tip still in adjustment window) is displayed with a 'Pending' indicator and excluded from the confirmed net earnings total."
    }

  "acceptance_criteria": [
    {
      "id": "AC-1",

    { "scenario": "A California driver completes zero deliveries but is on-shift (engaged to wait)", "expected_behavior": "Base pay for hours on-shift must still be displayed and must meet minimum wage. An earnings breakdown showing $0 for an employee who was on-shift is a wage violation." },
```
